title: Bridge Essay: Shared Neural Mechanisms of Visual Perception and Imagery.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Shared, Neural, Mechanisms, of, Visual.

**Shared Neural Mechanisms of Visual Perception and Imagery.** — Trends Cogn Sci. PMID: 30876729. DOI: 10.1016/j.tics.2019.02.004. For decades, the extent to which visual imagery relies on the same neural mechanisms as visual perception has been atopic of debate. Here, we review recent neuroimaging studies comparing these two forms of visual experience. Their results suggest that there is alarge overlap in neural processing during perception and imagery: neural representations of imagined and perceived stimuli are similar in the visual, parietal, and frontal cortex.
